# /solution-review

> Cross-agent gap analysis. Produces a structured report listing every inconsistency that crosses agent boundaries. Does NOT block downstream commands; surfaces gaps for the affected agent(s) to resolve. Per [constitution/02-aggregation-rules.md § /solution-review](../../constitution/02-aggregation-rules.md).

## Usage

```
/solution-review [--project <name>] [--severity blocker|warning|info]
```

| Flag | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `--project <name>` | Override the project resolved from cwd |
| `--severity <level>` | Filter findings to a minimum severity (default: all findings reported) |

## Inputs

Reads:

- Every domain agent's `fdd.md`, `tdd.md`, `blueprint.md` (per docScope)
- `project.config.yaml` for `agents-enabled` + NFR targets + ALM hierarchy
- `agents.yaml` for docScope keys per agent
- The unified `solution-blueprint.md` (if it exists; otherwise the source per-agent blueprints directly)

## Execution flow

1. Resolve project + read `project.config.yaml` + `agents.yaml`.
2. For each agent in `agents-enabled`, walk its docs per [02-aggregation-rules.md § Source discovery](../../constitution/02-aggregation-rules.md).
3. Run each finding category check (see [02-aggregation-rules.md § /solution-review gap analysis](../../constitution/02-aggregation-rules.md)):
   - `ENTITY-MISMATCH` — schema name + logical name + display name comparison across agent docs
   - `NFR-CONFLICT` — extract NFR targets per component; flag conflicts on the same end-to-end NFR
   - `INTEGRATION-CONTRACT-DRIFT` — for every cross-agent contract, compare consumer's expected schema vs producer's declared schema
   - `MISSING-UPSTREAM` — agents in `agents-enabled` with no blueprint.md
   - `OWNERSHIP-CONFLICT` — concerns claimed by multiple agents (Principle 1)
   - `ORPHAN-COMPONENT` — components referenced by nothing + producing nothing
   - `UNRECONCILED-ADR` — contradictory ADR citations across agents
4. Assemble findings into [templates/solution-review-report.template.md](../../templates/solution-review-report.template.md).
5. Apply `--severity` filter if passed.
6. Quality self-check inline; emit summary counts (blockers / warnings / info).
7. Write output to `projects/{p}/_aggregator/architecture/solution-review-report.md`.

## Output

- `projects/{p}/_aggregator/architecture/solution-review-report.md` (always)

## Severity legend

- **BLOCKER** — Cross-agent state is broken. Continuing without resolution will produce incorrect behaviour.
- **WARNING** — Inconsistency is non-functional but stakeholders should be aware.
- **INFO** — Notable observation.

## Behaviour notes

- This command does NOT change any per-agent doc. Findings are reported only.
- Re-running after the affected agent(s) fix their docs produces an updated report with fewer findings (idempotent).
- The report's severity counts are tracked in its frontmatter (`blockers`, `warnings`, `info`) for easy machine reads.
